current.summary: Install safety design complete — brainstorm + 2 rounds of adversarial panel review, all issues addressed. Ready for implementation planning.

activeWork.description: Install safety system for syllago — facts-only content verification at install time
activeWork.progress: Design doc is comprehensive and reviewed. Covers: quarantine fetch (StagingDir), behavioral scanner (3 scan levels, precision-first, trust model), GHSA feed integration, health signals (full 3-tier set, opportunistic Tier 1), TOCTOU integrity hashing, conditional install prompt UX, scanner version-aware update gates, 18 error codes, dependency auto-install. Two panel rounds: Round 1 had 4 reviewers (skeptic, security, pragmatist, pkg-mgr expert) — 3 critical + 5 moderate issues found and fixed. Round 2 had 2 reviewers — 5 refinements applied. Content hash deferred to research spike. Snyk dropped from v1 (subprocess trust). Private repos get same treatment as public.

git.lastCommit: a8933ca docs: ACP spec audit fixes

recentDecisions: Quarantine fetch: location-based isolation (StagingDir), bwrap/sandbox-exec optional for sandboxed scan
Private repos: same treatment as public (standard scan). Trust is always explicit via trustedSources config
Scan levels: 0 (skip), 1 (standard, default), 2 (strict, opt-in). Content-type-aware in standard mode
TOCTOU fix: per-file SHA-256 at scan time, re-verify full file manifest before move
Update gate: hold for approval when new findings appear (content changes only, not scanner version changes)
GHSA feed built-in (uses existing GitHub API). Snyk dropped from v1 due to subprocess trust concerns
Signal freshness: install always fetches fresh, TUI fetches fresh on launch then cached
Conditional install prompt: [Y/n] for clean, [d]/[i]/[n] for issues found. Truncate at 5 findings
Content hash deferred to separate research spike (normalization + cross-tool interop too complex for v1)
Typosquatting corpus: embedded attack data (SANDWORM_MODE) + registry-derived names at runtime
Scanner precision-first: match dangerous patterns not tool names. Small high-confidence initial set
macOS sandbox: sandbox-exec (deprecated but used by OpenAI, Google, Cursor — no alternative exists)
nextSteps: Invoke Plan skill to create implementation plan from design: /develop install-safety --resume
Implementation order: Scanner -> Quarantine Fetch -> Health Signals -> Feed Integration -> Dependency Auto-Install
The .develop/install-safety.json state file is ready with nextAction pointing to Plan skill

There are also 3 uncommitted research files from the previous session (16, 17, 18 in docs/research/agent-skills-spec/) — the previous handoff mentioned committing those but they may already be committed.
